name: ocimum sanctum (holy basil / tulsi)
mechanism: Constituents including eugenol and rosmarinic acid are reported to modulate inflammatory signaling and oxidative-stress pathways; adaptogenic effects are plausible but not fully resolved.
contraindications: use caution during pregnancy due to limited concentrated-extract safety data
monitor in people using glucose-lowering therapy
interactions: may add to glucose-lowering effects of antidiabetic medications
may modestly increase antiplatelet effects with blood-thinning drugs
safetyNotes: Moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician; Lower dose or stop if nausea, vomiting, or GI upset occurs.

sources.title: NCBI Taxonomy: ocimum sanctum (holy basil / tulsi)

description: Ocimum sanctum (holy basil / tulsi) is a medicinal herb traditionally used for reduction in perceived stress and mild glycemic support.
